hero.headline: MAHE Online MBA in Human Resource Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A++ | NIRF #3 University 2025 | Institution of Eminence | UGC-DEB Approved | Total fees ₹2,92,000

sections.tldr: MAHE Online MBA in HRM covers compensation and benefits, performance management, international HRM, training and development, talent management, and HR analytics. NAAC A++ NIRF #3 University with IoE tag. Total fee ₹2,92,000 (₹73,000/sem). Zero-cost EMI ₹12,167/month. Coursera Plus bundled free. 8 scholarship categories. Work experience required; fresh graduates submit SOP video.

about.body:
MAHE's Online MBA in Human Resource Management combines core people management practices with data-driven HR decision-making. Semester 3 covers compensation and benefits (salary structuring, statutory compliance, CTC design), performance management and appraisal (KRA/KPI frameworks, 360-degree feedback, bell curve debates), and international HRM (cross-cultural management, expatriate compensation, global mobility policies). Semester 4 adds training and development (TNA, L&D programme design, ROI of training), talent management and employee retention (EVP design, succession planning, attrition analytics), and HR analytics (people data dashboards, predictive attrition models, workforce planning).

The HR Analytics subject in Sem 4 is a genuine differentiator. Most competing HR MBAs treat analytics as a brief module, but MAHE offers it as a full subject with focus on predictive modelling and dashboard design.

MAHE holds NIRF #3 University 2025, NAAC A++, and Institution of Eminence status. Coursera Plus is bundled free. At ₹2,92,000, the fee is premium but the credential carries weight with HR departments at large corporates and MNCs that filter on university rank.

Work experience in reputed organisations is required. Fresh graduates can apply with an SOP video.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA management core. Semesters 3-4 below show the HRM specialisation subjects.

subjects.name: Management Accounting
subjects.desc: Cost analysis, budgeting, variance analysis, and management decision-making using accounting data.

subjects.name: Legal Aspects of Business
subjects.desc: Contract law, consumer protection, intellectual property, company law basics, and regulatory compliance.

subjects.name: Minor Project
subjects.desc: Applied HR research project supervised by faculty.

subjects.name: Compensation and Benefits
subjects.desc: Salary structuring, CTC design, statutory compliance (PF, ESI, Gratuity), incentive design, pay equity analysis, and global compensation benchmarking.

subjects.name: Performance Management and Appraisal
subjects.desc: KRA/KPI frameworks, balanced scorecard, 360-degree feedback, bell curve and forced ranking debates, OKR methodology, and PIP design.

subjects.name: International HRM
subjects.desc: Cross-cultural management, expatriate compensation and taxation, global mobility policies, international labour law, and managing geographically distributed teams.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship and Innovation
subjects.desc: Startup ideation, business model canvas, lean startup methodology, and funding strategies.

subjects.name: Sustainability
subjects.desc: Sustainable business models, ESG frameworks, and CSR strategy.

subjects.name: Capstone Project
subjects.desc: HR capstone integrating Sem 1-4 learning with an industry-linked organisational problem.

subjects.name: Training and Development
subjects.desc: Training needs analysis (TNA), L&D programme design, Kirkpatrick evaluation model, ROI of training, e-learning content development, and blended learning strategy.

subjects.name: Talent Management and Employee Retention
subjects.desc: Employee value proposition (EVP) design, succession planning, career pathing, attrition analysis, engagement surveys, and employer branding.

subjects.name: HR Analytics
subjects.desc: People data dashboards, predictive attrition models, workforce planning, recruitment funnel analytics, sentiment analysis, and data-driven HRBP decision-making.

whoHires.body:
MAHE MBA HRM graduates target roles across corporate HR, consulting, and HR tech:

**Industries:** IT services, BFSI, manufacturing, consulting, FMCG, pharma, e-commerce, and HR tech

**Entry-level roles:** HR Executive, Talent Acquisition Associate, Compensation Analyst, HRBP (junior), L&D Coordinator, Employee Engagement Associate

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** HR Manager, Talent Acquisition Manager, Compensation and Benefits Manager, L&D Manager, HR Analytics Lead, Employee Relations Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** CHRO, VP Human Resources, Head of Talent Acquisition, Director of L&D, Head of People Analytics, Global HR Director

**Employers:** IT firms (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCLTech), BFSI (HDFC Bank, ICICI, Kotak), consulting (Deloitte People Advisory, Mercer, Aon), FMCG (HUL, ITC, Nestle), and HR tech (Darwinbox, Keka, Zoho People).

**Note:** MAHE provides career support services. The Manipal alumni network is strong in healthcare, IT services, and banking sectors.

skills.body:
The HRM specialisation builds skills across people management, analytics, and organisational development:

**Technical skills:**
- Compensation Design: CTC structuring, statutory compliance mapping, incentive scheme modelling, and pay equity analysis
- HR Analytics: predictive attrition modelling, workforce planning with people data, recruitment funnel optimisation, and engagement survey analysis
- Performance Systems: KRA/KPI design, OKR implementation, 360-degree feedback administration, and PIP creation
- International HR: expatriate compensation calculation, cross-cultural assessment, and global mobility policy drafting

**Applied skills:**
- Training ROI: Kirkpatrick model application, training needs assessment, and blended learning programme design
- Talent Management: succession planning frameworks, EVP articulation, employer branding strategy, and retention programme design

**Soft skills:** Conducting difficult conversations (terminations, PIPs), negotiating with unions, presenting HR metrics to C-suite, and facilitating organisational change.

comparisons.body:
Comparing MAHE Online MBA HRM against peer programmes:

**vs MUJ Online MBA HRM:** MUJ at approximately ₹1,80,000 on the same platform. MAHE at ₹2,92,000 costs ₹1,12,000 more for the NIRF #3 and IoE brand. Both share similar LMS delivery. MAHE includes Coursera Plus. MUJ is not a downgrade in content. If your employer specifically values the MAHE flagship tag for HR leadership roles, the premium may be justified. Otherwise, MUJ offers strong value.

**vs Amity Online MBA HRM:** Amity at ₹2,25,000 with NIRF #22 and NAAC A++. MAHE at ₹2,92,000 costs ₹67,000 more but carries NIRF #3 (vs #22). Amity has a larger alumni base across HR roles. MAHE includes Coursera Plus; Amity does not.

**vs LPU Online MBA HRM:** LPU at approximately ₹1,60,000 with NIRF #19 and NAAC A++. Significantly cheaper. LPU has strong placement cell activity. MAHE wins on NIRF rank and IoE tag, but at nearly double the cost.

**vs DPU Pune Online MBA HRM:** DPU at ₹1,89,400 with NIRF #41 and NAAC A++. MAHE at ₹2,92,000 is substantially more expensive but carries NIRF #3 and IoE. DPU includes edX; MAHE includes Coursera Plus.

fees.body:
The MAHE Online MBA in Human Resource Management has a total programme fee of ₹2,92,000 inclusive of all taxes.

**Semester-wise fee breakdown:**
- Semester 1: ₹73,000
- Semester 2: ₹73,000
- Semester 3: ₹73,000
- Semester 4: ₹73,000
- **Total: ₹2,92,000**

**Application fee:** ₹500 one-time.

**Zero-cost EMI:** ₹12,167/month directly through the university.

**Scholarships (8 categories):**
- Defence Personnel / Ex-servicemen: 20%
- Divyaang / PwD: 20%
- Merit: 10% (75%+ aggregate)
- MAHE/AGE Alumni: 15%
- SAARC and EWC Nationals: 40%
- Female Learners: 15%
- Doctors: 15%
- Corporate Employees: 15%

**Important:** Learners opting for loan or financing are not eligible for additional discounts or offers.

**Bundled:** Coursera Plus access free with the programme.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with MAHE each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Human Resource Management as the specialisation, issued by Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A++ accreditation, NIRF #3 University rank, Institution of Eminence status, and WES recognition. Valid for corporate HR promotions, PSU roles, MNC HR leadership positions, and private-sector talent management recruitment. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from MAHE Online MBA HRM students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Sneha M.
items.liked: HR Analytics as a full subject, not just a module. Compensation and Benefits covered statutory compliance in practical depth
items.disliked: At ₹2,92,000, MUJ offers the same platform HRM MBA for ₹1,80,000. The MAHE premium needs better career support to justify
items.body: I picked MAHE for the NIRF #3 tag because my employer uses university rank as a filter for L5+ HR roles. The HR Analytics subject was the highlight, covering predictive attrition and workforce planning dashboards. Coursera Plus supplemented well with People Analytics courses from Wharton. The concern is value for money. MUJ delivers almost identical content on the same LMS. Unless the flagship brand specifically matters for your career, consider MUJ seriously.

items.name: Rajesh P.
items.liked: International HRM covered practical expatriate compensation and global mobility. Performance Management module addressed real PIP and bell curve scenarios
items.disliked: Career services were reactive, not proactive. Had to independently network through Manipal alumni groups for opportunities
items.body: Working in IT HR, the International HRM and Performance Management subjects were directly applicable. The Talent Management module on EVP design and employer branding was well taught. NIRF #3 and IoE cleared credential filters at two MNCs I applied to. The 15% corporate employee scholarship brought costs down to around ₹2,48,200, which made the decision easier versus full price.

items.name: Kavita R.
items.liked: Training and Development covered Kirkpatrick model in practical depth. Coursera Plus access gave supplementary L&D content
items.disliked: The loan-disqualifies-scholarship policy hurt. I needed financing but lost the 15% female learner discount, making effective cost higher than planned
items.body: The HRM curriculum is solid, especially the Sem 4 combination of T&D, Talent Management, and HR Analytics. My main frustration was the scholarship policy. I qualified for the 15% female learner scholarship but needed a loan for the remaining amount. MAHE's policy disqualifies all scholarships if you take financing. This pushed my effective cost to the full ₹2,92,000. Other universities like Amity and LPU allow scholarship plus loan combinations.

faqs.question: Does the HRM MBA include HR Analytics?
faqs.answer: Yes. HR Analytics is a full Sem 4 subject covering people data dashboards, predictive attrition models, workforce planning, and recruitment funnel analytics. This is deeper coverage than most competing HRM MBAs offer.

faqs.question: How does MAHE HRM compare to MUJ HRM?
faqs.answer: Same platform, similar delivery. MAHE at ₹2,92,000 carries NIRF #3 and IoE. MUJ at ₹1,80,000 costs ₹1,12,000 less. Choose MAHE if the flagship brand specifically matters. Choose MUJ if fee value is the priority.

faqs.question: Is work experience mandatory for admission?
faqs.answer: Work experience in reputed organisations is required. Fresh graduates without work experience can apply by submitting an SOP video as part of the application process.

faqs.question: Can loan applicants get scholarships?
faqs.answer: No. Learners opting for loan or financing are not eligible for additional discounts or offers. This is a critical consideration when comparing effective costs across programmes.

faqs.question: What is the total fee?
faqs.answer: ₹2,92,000 total (₹73,000 per semester). ₹500 application fee. Zero-cost EMI at ₹12,167/month. 8 scholarship categories available (up to 40%). Reconfirm with our counsellor before payment.

chooseIf: NIRF #3 and Institution of Eminence credential matters for MNC HR leadership role filters.
You want HR Analytics as a full subject, not a brief module in a broader HRM curriculum.
Coursera Plus access for People Analytics and L&D supplementary courses adds value.
You qualify for a scholarship category that reduces the ₹2,92,000 effective fee.
consider: MUJ at ₹1,80,000 offers similar platform delivery for ₹1,12,000 less.
Amity at ₹2,25,000 has a larger HR alumni network and costs less.
Loan financing disqualifies all scholarships, making MAHE one of the most expensive online MBA options.
